text: Terry, I think I gotta file my first grievance — supervisor wrote up Reggie for stepping over the line marker. Where do I even start?
First thing: don't argue it on the floor. Get the write-up, the incident report, and the supervisor sign-off sheet. Compare the timestamps. That's usually where it falls apart.
It's me they wrote up. The report says 2:15 but I was on break til 2:30, got the badge swipe to prove it.
That badge swipe is your whole case. Pull it, screenshot it, and write the timeline plain — no opinions, just times. Send me the draft before you file.
On it. I'll have the draft to you tonight. Appreciate you walking me through it.
Dumb question — what's Article 14 cover again? Steward in Zone 2 keeps quoting it at me.
Not a dumb question. Article 14 is call-in pay and reporting time. If they send you home early they still owe you the guarantee. Read the whole section, not just the part they quote.
So if they call me in and there's no work, I still get paid the four hours?
Right, unless it's an act-of-God shutdown. Keep the language handy, management 'forgets' it constantly.
Anybody cover second shift Thursday? Got a hearing prep with Terry I can't move.
I got you Antoine. Just leave me the open-case list before you go.
Good. Vanessa, if anything comes up on the line you can't handle, call me, don't sit on it. Better to ask than to let it cool.
We got Marcus W. from temp to perm today!! 9 months on the line. Thank you Terry for the documentation push.
That's the win. You documented every shift and start date — that's what carried it, not me. Now you teach the next one how to do it.
Congrats Marcus! Can we get the documentation checklist pinned somewhere?
I'll post the steward checklist in the Training group tonight. Everybody new should run through it.
Posted the new-steward checklist: get the paperwork, check timestamps, get it in writing, never argue on the floor. Next training session is the 24th at the hall, 6pm.
I'll be there. Can you cover how to handle a member who doesn't want to file even when they've got a case?
Good topic. Short answer: it's their choice, your job is to make sure they know their rights, not to file for them. We'll role-play it Thursday.
Adding it to the agenda. Bringing donuts.
